summary: Deadlock in topology worker shutdown when SDAM monitor is streaming
description:
h3. Summary

{{Client::shutdown()}} can deadlock when an SDAM monitor is running in streaming mode. The topology worker's shutdown sequence waits for the monitor to exit, while the monitor is blocked waiting for the topology worker to acknowledge an update.
h3. Reproduction

The bug requires the monitor to complete an awaitable hello and call {{topology_updater.update()}} in the narrow window between the topology worker exiting its processing loop and calling {{{}close_monitor(){}}}. This is difficult to trigger in pure Rust but has been reproduced when the driver is used via FFI with a shared tokio runtime (e.g., a Java/Panama wrapping driver), and in pure Rust by adding a Thread::sleep.
h3. Root Cause

When {{Client::shutdown()}} is called:

The {{TopologyWorker}} receives {{{}Broadcast(Shutdown){}}}, broadcasts to connection pools, and breaks out of its event processing loop.

It enters the cleanup sequence: drops the publisher, then calls {{close_monitor().await}} on each server's {{{}MonitorManager{}}}.

{{close_monitor()}} drops the {{{}WorkerHandle{}}}, sends {{{}CancellationReason::ServerClosed{}}}, and awaits {{cancellation_sender.closed()}} — waiting for the monitor task to fully exit.

Meanwhile, the monitor (running in streaming mode) may have just received a response to its awaitable hello. It processes the reply and calls {{{}self.topology_updater.update(server_description).await{}}},
which sends an {{UpdateMessage}} on the unbounded channel and awaits acknowledgment via a oneshot.

The topology worker's {{update_receiver}} is still alive (it's a field on the worker struct), so the send succeeds and the message is buffered. But the worker has already exited its loop and will never recv from the channel again. The oneshot acknowledgment never arrives.

Circular wait:
 - The topology worker is in {{{}close_monitor().await{}}}, waiting for the monitor's cancellation receiver to be dropped (i.e., the monitor task to exit).
 - The monitor task is in {{{}topology_updater.update().await{}}}, waiting for the topology worker to acknowledge its message.

Neither can make progress.
h3. Fix

Drop {{self.update_receiver}} after breaking out of the processing loop, before calling {{{}close_monitor(){}}}. This causes the {{UnboundedSender::send()}} in the monitor's {{update()}} call to return {{Err}}(receiver gone), so {{send_message()}} returns {{false}} immediately. The monitor proceeds, checks {{is_alive()}} (which returns false because {{close_monitor}} dropped the handle), and exits.

Dropping (rather than just closing) the receiver also drops any already-buffered messages, which drops their oneshot senders, unblocking any monitors that already sent an update and are waiting for acknowledgment.
h3. Behavioral Impact

Minimal. During the brief shutdown window, a monitor's final {{ServerDescriptionChanged}} SDAM event may be dropped (the topology description won't be updated from that last heartbeat). All shutdown lifecycle events ({{{}ServerClosed{}}}, {{{}TopologyDescriptionChanged{}}}, {{{}TopologyClosed{}}}) are unaffected — they are emitted directly in the shutdown sequence, not through the update channel.

summary: Collection management unified test is failing against latest server build
description:
[Example failure|https://parsley.corp.mongodb.com/test/mongo_rust_driver_rhel_8_test_latest_standalone_patch_f20b3d040822cf95cbd40412fbe2ae0ea8e003af_69e624d689d17200075c579a_26_04_20_13_06_35/0/1bda5f3bdb71d7fba3f236f0941620b4?bookmarks=0,265&shareLine=0]; the test in question is in {{clustered-indexes.yml}}, ["listIndexes returns the index"|https://github.com/mongodb/specifications/blob/master/source/collection-management/tests/clustered-indexes.yml#L101].  It looks like the server is now returning an additional {{collation}} field that's causing the match to fail.

Interesting, it looks like this isn't failing in other drivers (C# at least has confirmed it's seeing the additional field but the test is still passing) so this might be something wrong in how our matcher works.

summary: Refactor to work with the new Async Observables
description:
New Observable style classes were added in JAVA-1805

So the core driver no longer needs the reactive streams dependency.

summary: Prefer Seq over List for the API
description: Lists are a type of Seq but Seq's are easier to construct and have the same requirements.

summary: Quick Tour
Refactor Bson interaction
description:
Make it easier to create {{BsonValues}} via aliases and companion objects
Refactor {{Bson}} implicits - currently loses typesafety
Rename core scala sbt project to Bson

summary: Bson guide
description: Copy the Java Bson Guide but apply to the Scala driver.

summary: Use constant-time hash comparison functions
Fix MongoClient.apply(URI)
description: Needs cleaning up - no need to create a whole new object.

summary: Builders Documentation
description: Based off the Java docs

summary: Reference Guide
Bring names in WriteConcern to spec
Creating MongoClient with MongoClientSettings builder does not include Scala codec's DEFAULT_CODEC_REGISTRY
description:
Creating a client using a URI sets the default codec registry to org.mongodb.scala.MongoClient.DEFAULT_CODEC_REGISTRY, but building a MongoClient with MongoClientSettings.builder() defaults to com.mongodb.async.client.MongoClients.DEFAULT_CODEC_REGISTRY.

This causes a CodecConfigurationException (org.bson.codecs.configuration.CodecConfigurationException) to be thrown when trying to use Documents (immutable or mutable).

Also, there's a duplicate definition for the DEFAULT_CODEC_REGISTRY, both in MongoClient and in the org.mongodb.scala.bson.codecs package object.

summary: Improve WriteConcern API
description: Make chainable eg: {{WriteConcern().journaled()}}

summary: Support InsertOne with InsertOne Options
Update Aggregate helpers to mirror Java 3.2 helpers
Add support for IndexOptionDefaults when creating a collection
Change showDiskLoc to showRecordId
CreateIndex is not creating unique index with unique.true paramater
Should handle closed clients gracefully
description: If a client is closed and some data is requested - errors should be passed via the callback

summary: Drivers must raise an error if response messageLength > ismaster.maxMessageSizeBytes
Work better with Future
Changing result of flatMap to Future throws "Promise already completed"
description:
There is some problem with changing result of flatMap to future:
{code}
val x = Observable(List("a"))
val y = Observable(List("b"))

val observable: Observable[String] = x.flatMap(x ⇒ y)
observable.toFuture()
{code}

Code from above results in:
{code}
Promise already completed.
java.lang.IllegalStateException: Promise already completed.
	at scala.concurrent.Promise$class.complete(Promise.scala:55)
	at scala.concurrent.impl.Promise$DefaultPromise.complete(Promise.scala:153)
	at scala.concurrent.Promise$class.success(Promise.scala:86)
	at scala.concurrent.impl.Promise$DefaultPromise.success(Promise.scala:153)
	at org.mongodb.scala.ObservableImplicits$ScalaObservable$$anonfun$toFuture$1.apply(ObservableImplicits.scala:332)
	at org.mongodb.scala.ObservableImplicits$ScalaObservable$$anonfun$toFuture$1.apply(ObservableImplicits.scala:332)
	at org.mongodb.scala.ObservableImplicits$ScalaObservable$$anon$1.onNext(ObservableImplicits.scala:88)
	at org.mongodb.scala.internal.MapObservable$$anon$1.onNext(MapObservable.scala:32)
	at org.mongodb.scala.internal.FoldLeftObservable$$anon$1.onComplete(FoldLeftObservable.scala:35)
	at org.mongodb.scala.internal.FlatMapObservable$$anon$1.onComplete(FlatMapObservable.scala:71)
	at org.mongodb.scala.internal.IterableObservable$$anon$1.request(IterableObservable.scala:46)
	at org.mongodb.scala.internal.FlatMapObservable$$anon$1$$anon$2.request(FlatMapObservable.scala:54)
	at org.mongodb.scala.ObservableImplicits$ScalaObservable$$anon$1.onSubscribe(ObservableImplicits.scala:86)
	at org.mongodb.scala.internal.MapObservable$$anon$1.onSubscribe(MapObservable.scala:28)
	at org.mongodb.scala.internal.FoldLeftObservable$$anon$1.onSubscribe(FoldLeftObservable.scala:32)
	at org.mongodb.scala.internal.FlatMapObservable$$anon$1.onSubscribe(FlatMapObservable.scala:66)
...
{code}

I'm using mongo-scala-driver 1.1.0 and MongoDB server 3.2.3.

In debug it looks like onNext is executed once again after onComplete.

summary: Deprecate "textSearchEnabled" server parameter
Ensure Observables can be subscribed to multiple times
description: The IterableObserver should not set the initial state of the iterable on each subscription, so it can be subscribed to multiple times.

summary: ConnectionString doesn't work with connectTimeoutMS
description:
I try to reduce the connection timeout because default is 30s (It's very long).

val mongoClient: MongoClient = MongoClient("mongodb://localhost/?connectTimeoutMS=500&socketTimeoutMS=500")

When I stopped the mongo server and I try to connect I have this message 30s after :
MongoTimeoutException: Timed out after 30000 ms while waiting for a server that matches WritableServerSelector

summary: Inc is incrementing twice with upsert
Explain in modifiers ignored when connected to 3.2 server
Some connection string options not applied.
description:
MongoClients does not apply the following connection string properties to the MongoClient:

* readConcern
* writeConcern
* readPreference

Because of this, the MongoClient ends up with the default for each of these.

summary: Create Codec for native Scala types
insertOne not accepting mutable Document
description:
Running the example from: https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-scala-driver/blob/master/examples/src/test/scala/tour/QuickTour.scala

import org.mongodb.scala.bson.collection.mutable.Document
import org.mongodb.scala.{MongoClient, MongoCollection, MongoDatabase}

val mongoClient: MongoClient = MongoClient()
val database: MongoDatabase = mongoClient.getDatabase("mydb")
val collection: MongoCollection[Document] = database.getCollection("test")

Method {color:red}.results(){color} does not exist anymore, therefore unable to "wait-for results". Observer does not appear to work with mutable type either

Referenced http://mongodb.github.io/mongo-scala-driver/1.1/getting-started/quick-tour/#insert-a-document

Unable to get the _id back once inserted. Is this a bug?

collection.drop(){color:red}.results(){color}

summary: The 2.11 pom file contains a dependency on the scala reflection 2.12 library
description:
The Scala driver has a dependency on scala-reflect that was never marked as a test dependency but appears to have been.

However, since updating the ScalaTest and ScalaMock its no longer a required as an explicit dependency and can be removed.

summary: Create a 2.12.0 Build
description: Depends On: https://github.com/paulbutcher/ScalaMock/issues/119

summary: MongoCollection methods should default to the collection type.
description: The find method implicitly defaults to {{Document}} rather than {{TResult}}. This should not be the case.

summary: Collect not working after flatMap
Investigate SingleObservable class
description:
RxJava and others now have a special {{Observable}} type that explicitly indicates that methods return an {{Observable}} of 1.

Then implicits like {{toFuture}} can be coerced to return the correct type eg: {{Future[Document]}} or {{Future[Seq[Document]]}} depending on {{Observable}} type.

summary: Improve Observables error message
description:
Currently, if an Observer doesn't call {{onSubscribe}} the {{FlatMapObservable}} errors with: {{java.util.NoSuchElementException: None.get at scala.None$.get(Option.scala:344)}}.

Lets explicitly check state in the Observables and give a helpful error message if {{onNext}} is called before {{onSubscribe}}.

summary: Add feature to customize Observables returned by driver
description: Similar to JAVARX-15 - allow work on the Observables to be passed into a different thread pool or context.

summary: Harden the CaseClassCodec when finding null values.
description:
With the {{case class Person(name: String)}} encoding {{Person(null)}} will throw a NPE. 

If though its heretical Scala to create such an instance, lets make it throw a {{CodecConfigurationException}} instead.

summary: Add support for CaseClasses to not encode None values.
description: Relies on SCALA-299 handling the ability to hydrate Case Classes without keys.

summary: Support type aliases in the CaseClassCodec
description: Currently, they aren't supported and you can't create a codec from case classes containing an alias as it won't compile.

summary: Support extra fields in document when decoding Case Classes
Case Class Codecs should support Java Map implementations like BsonDocument
description: Will have to use the codec registry to find a codec for the implementation.

summary: Support Case Classes with val attributes in them
description: See PR: https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-scala-driver/pull/31

summary: flatMap sets requests to MAX_VALUE if source doesn't support backpressure
